subject: [AAI-2177] Run container process as non-root

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/aai-gizmo-elalto-verify-java/1/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/aai-gizmo-elalto-verify-java/1

Patch Set 1:

Hi All,

Requesting you to review my changes for the Non-root feature in Gizmo.
(previously this was checked in to dublin branch by mistake [https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/aai/gizmo/+/91808] - it is abandoned by me now).

Some clarifications:
1. Are we aligned with the gid and uid that the other services are using (for example resources and traversal)?  I believe they also run as the aaiadmin user.  It could cause problems if they have a different uid and gid, as the aaiadmin user needs to be configured on the host machine.

Answer: Note that as per the discussion on the wiki discussion, there is no pre-req of an existing user on host machine to be mapped to docker. 


2. Line 38:
What is this logs directory used for?  When I look at the logback file, it seems we are writing the logs to /var/log/onap.  Is anything getting written to /logs?
Answer: The /logs is a symbolic link as per the existing arrangement.However, a corresponding folder needs to exist on docker in order to allow for the same.

Regards,
Rajeev
Change has been successfully merged by Steven Blimkie

Patch Set 1:
